tests.description: initScene creates a THREE.WebGLRenderer instance (not Canvas2D or any other backend)
No Canvas2D rendering context (getContext('2d') / CanvasRenderingContext2D) is used in any src/visual/ module
initScene returns a PerspectiveCamera that is added to the scene graph (instanceof THREE.PerspectiveCamera)
Camera has a perspective projection (camera.isPerspectiveCamera === true, fov > 0)
When WebGL is unavailable, initScene throws a WebGLUnavailableError (not a generic Error)
When WebGL is unavailable, a user-facing fallback message is rendered into the container instead of a canvas
When WebGL is unavailable, no render loop, audio, or pointer systems are initialized
The fallback message container has dark styling (black background, light text) consistent with the site aesthetic
No source files under src/ import or reference CanvasRenderingContext2D or call getContext('2d') as a primary render path
Production build succeeds with no TypeScript errors (npm run build)
